items.title: Jubilee, or Jubi Lee (Korean Man)
items.summary: I write these musings to clear my brain. It was, for…
items.content_html:
I write these musings to clear my brain.

It was, for quite a long time, perhaps still is, cluttered and messy.

Like an old(ish) attic.

Full of dusty memories.

Long forgotten ideas.

And opinions bottled up to ferment like a good kimchi.

Or booch.

You know, kombucha?

Booch is a fun word to say.

Booch.

Tee hee.

I've gone in this attic many times since I started this thing.

Bringing things out into the daylight.

Cleaning and clearing up a bit.

Releasing them out into the wild.

Freeing them.

It occurs to me that I have started to repeat myself in these rambles.

That I have multiples of things I treasure.

Redundancies cubed.

3 is 1...

As one should.

As four should.

That I hammer home certain points.

Over and over.

Pounding 'em.

When I don't know what I'm going to write about (which happens to be the majority of the time), I tend to ramble.

Then I read it and think, "Hmm, I wrote about this before."

I suppose that's the point of iterating.

Viewpoint.

Refine.

Repeat.

Improve.

I have good days and bad days now.

As we all do.

My bads suck, but they aren't all bad.

There are always golden linings.

Not all bad.

A lot of good.

Just like me.

And hopefully, you, too.

What does this make possible?

Thank you for coming to my attic.

Why are you in my attic?

I hope you find what you're looking for.

I know I have.

items.title: All The Worlds
items.summary: As a fan of old(ish) professional wrestling, you start to see…
items.content_html:
As a fan of old(ish) professional wrestling, you start to see the world as a work.

Work is a term used in PW to describe something that is prearranged.

Some call it fake, but that's not necessarily a bad thing.

Everything is staged.

If not rehearsed, it is at least discussed. 

Prepared for.

Sure there's chaos.

Randomness.

But steps typically lead up to it.

That were talked about.

Practiced.

There's an old joke I heard about a guy who tried to get into Shakespeare.

He read all of Billy's works and just didn't really like it.

Why?

He explained, "It's too full of cliches."

...

Laugh.

Where was I?

Have you ever willed something into existence?

I mean, set a fantastic goal, an almost impossible one, and achieved it?

Things always look so possible after the fact.

Looking back.

You start stacking wins, and the impossible seems not only possible, but probable.

Inevitable.

Fake it till you make it.

Right.

If you fake it and you make it, was it fake?

I'm not sure.

And does fake just mean lying?

Must you lie to "make" it?

Be real.

Tell the truth.

Do they?

Don't hate the players. 

Love the game.

Your move.

And...action!

🎬

items.title: Some Say It Is So
items.summary: Speak it into existence. Go ahead. Say what you have been…
items.content_html:
Speak it into existence.

Go ahead.

Say what you have been aiming for.

I don't mean in your head.

I mean out your mouth.

Lemme hear it.

Too loud.

Whisper it.

Spoken words are powerful.

And sometimes, the calmest words said are the fieriest.

Are the finest.

So speak easy.

Let me hear you.

Are you the one?

items.title: Iron
items.summary: "Listen. This is what I learned: Everybody that you fight Is…
items.content_html:
"Listen.

This is what I learned:

Everybody that you fight

Is not your enemy.

And everybody that helps you

Is not your friend."

🥊

items.title: Capital i Looks Like Lowercase L
items.summary: One of the most profitable gimmicks to come along in a…
items.content_html:
One of the most profitable gimmicks to come along in a bit is the predictions markets.

Betting on today's high in local weather.

Seriously.

And it turns out, one of the very best ones sort of faked their success.

Or at least, the success of its users.

A fake it till you make it situation.

And it got me to thinking.

Uh oh.

If gambling sites have to fake it to make it, what does that mean?

Gambling it would seem would be the least necessary to do anything other than say "Give us money, we'll give you odds."

If you have to fake a winning, even though you're legit, what the heck is going on?

And you know what I came up with?

I don't know if it's a logical conclusion but here it is.

Everything is marketing.

Even bad news.

Even controversy.

Pay the fine and move on.

Bigger than ever.

Worth it.

And also, everyone is selling you something.

Even the free sample person at a warehouse store.

That sheet is free with a string.

Even your friends and fam.

"If you join as a distributor, you get an even bigger discount!"

It doesn't mean selling is a bad thing, which I suppose I sometimes still believe.

My idea of selling is to just be me (sometimes turned to 11) and let people ask to buy.

If you get me, you get it.

No understand me, no understand it.

No big whoop.

But that is selling.

Because we always are.

Except me.

I ain't selling sheet.

NFS.

...

But just out of curiosity, what are we talking here?

💰💰💰

Pass.

items.title: Sunset's Faster
items.summary: I want to say a lot of things today. I really…
items.content_html:
I want to say a lot of things today.

I really do.

But I won't.

Because I don't want to.

Make sense?

No, I suppose it doesn't.

Summer's started.

But it was already sorta here.

June's like that.

Starting.

But already here.

Historically, it's a time to sit back.

Go on vacation.

Other than love, character, and faith, I think the most important thing left to teach is about money.

Defense of self and others, too, I suppose that protects all of the above.

I once heard this standup comedian talk about his horrible credit.

He went on and on about it.

About his credit report.

How he got a physical copy of it.

And how bad it was.

How it didn't stop.

Just went on and on.

And how he didn't care.

"What they gonna do, add a page?"

Shoot.

That had me rolling.

Thank God for wisdom.

But man, that sheet was funny.

items.title: Make My Day
items.summary: Today's my day. As far as days go. Every day is.
items.content_html:
Today's my day.

As far as days go.

Every day is.

My day, that is.

More so today?

Some would like us to think so.

I didn't always get to celebrate today.

At least, not as the center of it.

But I have now for quite some time.

And here's the thing.

As for most holidays and "special occasions."

Every day is my birthday.

You ARE old.

Every day is Father's Day.

Every day is a holiday.

Special.

It just so happens that my loved ones find today a cause to celebrate.

And who am I to say no to a party?

Within reason.

Father is one of my favorite titles.

Might be the most, but I don't want the other titles to feel bad.

I have to do that now, as a father, you see.

No favorites.

All favorites.

Tied for first, certainly.

It's such an important title they made a stationary card day dedicated to honoring it.

Just like moms the month before.

Mamas come first.

As it should be.

And so, today's my day.

Has been for a while, now.

Just like every other day.

So very special.

Thank you.

Now, let's party!!!

Within reason.

Get down off that couch.

Got dang too loud.

Turn that lower.

You ok?

What was that?

Is that a bag of almond M&M's?

For me?

Re-sealable?

Woo-hoo!

My kinda party.

items.title: Save The World
items.summary: 🎵 "We are the world. "We are the children." 🎶 When…
items.content_html:
🎵 "We are the world.

"We are the children." 🎶

When you're a beginner, you want to get right to the heart of the matter.

You have no chill.

Just jumping to conclusions.

Leaping to them.

When you should just chill.

This is a frame that applies to most everything in life.

And it will serve you well if you heed.

If you heed.

Chill.

Relax.

Patience.

You want the steak dinner, you got to have salad first.

Maybe some bread.

Soup.

A tasty beverage.

Small talk.

What are you talking about? You only eat steak!

Grandmaster can break all rules.

That's why he make rules.

For the exceptions.

One of the wisest boxing instructors I learned from talked about rhythm.

How important it is.

And how you have to learn it.

How you have to master it.

1-2-3.

1-2-3.

1-2-3.

Art is dance.

It's martial dancing, really.

And, how you spend years learning the rhythmic patterns.

But, and this is a big but as far as buts are concerned, you only become a true master when you learn to break rhythm.

Mastery by learning the basics, perfecting them, and then interweaving the exact opposite techniques.

1-3-2.

3-1-2.

8-6-7-5-3-0-9.

You break the dance.

You know who's unpredictable?

Who never had rhythm?

The beginner.

The noob.

I always thought that kind of funny.

You spend years mastering something.

Having it down to a science.

But then, having become a bit too predictable, you have to sort of channel your inner noob.

To go back to the beginning.

Where it all started.

To truly get to the heart of the master.

🌉

🎵 "Save the world

We never wanted.

We never wanted

To save the world.

We never wanted

To have to save ourselves." 🎶

🎸

No masters here, though.

Only grands.

Pianos that is.

That I tickle in my mind.

Tickle in my mind.

Tee hee.

Tee hee.

But I repeat myself.

Save yourself.

Save the world.

But I repeat myself.

🐺

items.title: Proof of Future
items.summary: As an old(ish) man who plays the long game, it's hard…
items.content_html:
As an old(ish) man who plays the long game, it's hard to plan for such things in the modern world, particularly so in technology.

Like, say you want to future proof your tech situation.

Your ecosystem.

Get ahead of things.

You know what the term "future proof" gets you in tech?

About five to seven years.

Tops.

What do I mean?

Say you want to buy a laptop.

But you don't buy the base model.

You want it to be relevant and capable for a long time.

You want to get ahead of all the base heads.

I'm talking about bass!

So you start clicking upgrades.

Like a fiend.

More cores.

More RAM.

More storage.

Ouch, ouch, ouch!

Triple ouch.

Thanks, AI.

But, you reason, it'll be future proof!

Redundancy is a good plan for future proofing, but it doesn't stack in tech.

You'll just have more outdated crap.

Eventually.

So tech is disposable.

Like toilet paper.

Also a tech, back in its day.

You mean I don't have to use tree leaves anymore?

Utility off the charts.

But disposable.

Lots of things are like that.

All of which begs the question:

What isn't?

What is really non-disposable?

What is truly future proof?

While you're pondering that, you might want to lock in a laptop.

For the next 5 to 7.

💻

Them data centers aren't going to build themselves.

Are they!??!?!

items.title: Like Caine In Kung Fu
items.summary: Words are important. And as some of you may know, one…
items.content_html:
Words are important.

And as some of you may know, one of my favorite words is actualization.

I usually capitalize it.

Actualization.

It's a big word.

But it has BIG meaning.

Several, in fact, as the best words do.

The one I like best is that you take this idea and turn it into reality.

Through action.

What once wasn't real is now so.

Creating truth.

Of course, the best known definition is the process of achieving one's fullest potential.

Although we usually throw a "self" in front of it.

But I think actualization is more than just the self.

It's about going beyond self.

Sure, sure, be all that you can be.

Spend a whole life doing that. 

But then what?

Then what?

And as selfish as I am, have been, will be, this is my life's mission.

"What happened here is a miracle. And I want you to bleepin' acknowledge it!"

To drop the self.

To then what.

I'm not saying I have achieved actualization.

"From here on in, consider my arse retired."

With or without the self.

"You read the Bible, Ringo?"

"Not regularly, no."

But I'm trying, Ringo.

I'm trying.

Real.

Hard.

Man, you haven't tried at all!

To be the shepherd.

Go.

🥋

"What is a miracle, Vincent?"

"Act of God."

"And what's an Act of God?"

"When, umm, God makes the impossible possible. But this morning, I don't think, qualifies."
